notes: Rebaselined after Wave-3 Pacejka work. Three fidelity-improving changes drive the drift: (a) the grip_chain now folds inside the friction-ellipse cap instead of multiplying after, so combined forces no longer exceed the physical friction circle by ~2.5×; (b) ERS deploy uses an electric-motor torque-limited force-boost curve (F = min(F_max, P/v)) instead of a 40 m/s reference-speed floor, so launch boost is no longer ~4× too low; (c) per-axis ellipse uses `(fx/μx)² + (fy/μy)² ≤ 1` instead of an isotropic circle. Net effect: 0-100 km/h dropped 4.48 → 3.09s (close to real F1 ~2.6 s; legacy 3.68 s was already unrealistically slow under PEAK_TORQUE_NM 480 + clamp 40 m/s); stop distances slightly shorter (friction cap binding correctly).
